title: Ensure subnets are associated with network security groups

input_row.title: Ensure subnets are associated with network security groups
input_row.description: Protect subnet resources by ensuring subnets are associated with network security groups, which can filter inbound and outbound traffic using security rules.
input_row.rationale: Unprotected subnets can expose resources to unauthorized access.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Virtual networks. 2. Click the name of a virtual network. 3. Under Settings, click Subnets. 4. Click the name of a subnet. 5. Under Security, ensure Network security group is not set to None. 6. Repeat steps 1-5 for each virtual network and subnet. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list virtual networks: az network vnet list For each virtual network, run the following command to list subnets: az network vnet show --resource-group --name --query subnets For each subnet, run the following command to get the network security group id: az network vnet subnet show --resource-group --vnet-name --name --query networkSecurityGroup.id Ensure a network security group id is returned. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: e71308d3-144b-4262-b144-efdc3cc90517 - Name: 'Subnets should be associated with a Network Security Group'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Virtual networks. 2. Click the name of a virtual network. 3. Under Settings, click Subnets. 4. Click the name of a subnet. 5. Under Security, next to Network security group, click None to display the drop-down menu. 6. Select a network security group. 7. Click Save. 8. Repeat steps 1-7 for each virtual network and subnet requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each subnet requiring remediation, run the following command to associate it with a network security group: az network vnet subnet update --resource-group --vnet-name --name --network-security-group
input_row.impact: Minor administrative effort is required to ensure subnets are associated with network security groups. There is no cost to create or use network security groups.
input_row.default_value: By default, a subnet is not associated with a network security group.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ec2_subnet_network_security_group_association_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the following AWS API calls or boto3 SDK methods:
  - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_subnets()` to enumerate all subnets in the account and verify their association with network security groups.
  
- AWS CLI commands:
  - `aws ec2 describe-subnets --query "Subnets[*].[SubnetId,NetworkAcls,GroupId]"` to list subnets and their associated Network ACLs and security groups.

- Specific resources to enumerate:
  - All subnets across all VPCs in the target AWS account.

- Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:
  - For each subnet returned, check that the `GroupId` (Network Security Group ID) is not null or equivalent to a default indication of no association (e.g., an empty string or 'None').

- Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:
  - **Pass:** If the `GroupId` is present and is not set to 'None' for each subnet.
  - **Fail:** If any subnet has no associated network security group (GroupId is null or 'None'). 

This approach ensures a systematic audit of the networking configurations to confirm that all subnets are secured by relevant network security groups.
